content:
Hello,

Problem encountered with on 3.0.5 on iOS (last stable). The thing is that I use a Webview with a isSecureTextEntry added in Swift to prevent screenshot... I don't knowif the problem can come from the webview OR/AND the securetextfield (I don't think so imho), but maybe that can help.

We observed the flickering too, hard to reproduce but it definitive happens. Tried all v3.0.x releases (up to v3.0.5) - all show the same flickering issue (using google play releases). On the video one flickering is visible - but all parts of the application can flicker.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/837347/183376511-58bc6766-4020-441f-8673-1bfc327e1982.mp4

you issue is unrelated. the flickering in your case is due to fast changing text with different width glyphs.

> you issue is unrelated. the flickering in your case is due to fast changing text with different width glyphs.

The fast changing text is just one effect, sometimes the background color is flickering too. This happens rarely and after switching screens pause/resume app the issue gone. 

Edit: I update the code, I'm pretty sure Flutter shows for some reason and old buffer that contains an old time (using `print` and seconds in the time to trigger the issue much more). It is definitive triggered when I wake up my Android phone.

Edit2: When I add `key: UniqueKey()` to the text widget, I cannot reproduce the error anymore!

We use a custom `MainActivity.java` file, in the `onCreate` function we use this code to get a transparent background:

`getIntent().putExtra(INTENT_EXTRA_BACKGROUND_MODE, transparent.toString());` 

-> when we remove this line, no flickering is visible. does anyone else use a transparent background of the main window?

Hi guys, I just want to confirm some behaviors we have seen before:

An user reported he opened the app from the background and then he tried to go to the iPhone's home screen swiping up, but then the app started to flicker and when the flickering stopped the app was frozen.

It seems to me that the key here is something happening when the app was back from background and then going back to background.

> It seems to me that the key here is something happening when the app was back from background and then going back to background.

I forgot to mention that but yes, from our side, we reproduced this issue only twice, and it was always when I goes back to my app from the background, and navigate a little bit before the flickering happens, every time on iOS.

I tried to reproduce this bug in debug mode, with no success...

> Hi guys, I just want to confirm some behaviors we have seen before:
> 
> An user reported he opened the app from the background and then he tried to go to the iPhone's home screen swiping up, but then the app started to flicker and when the flickering stopped the app was frozen.
> 
> It seems to me that the key here is something happening when the app was back from background and then going back to background.

There are two situations on my side, which should be related to the ios version, 1. Open the app, swipe up, then get stuck, the app flicker, 2. Return to the app from the background and start flicker
这个问题我们也遇到过多次，通常是在应用进入后台后，过几分钟再唤醒过出现的。有一次是连着Xcode调试时出现的，Xcode打出的错误是GPU卡死，希望可以尽快修复。

This is the research summary of our app's flickering matter. This summary is only from our developers team, not from users. **Summary (last month):** | Number of Repetitions | Devices | iOS version | Xcode version | Flutter version | | ---- | ---- |---- |---- |---- | | 15 | in most iPhones | 15.x, 16 | 13.4.1 | 3.0.4 or more | **Matching of Device & iOS version:** | Device | iOS version | | ---- | ---- | | iPhone 12 mini | 15.5 | | iPhone 13 Pro | 15.4.1 | | iPhone 12 Pro | 16 | | iPhone SE 2nd | 15.1 | | iPhone XR | 15.5 | | iPhone 12 pro | 15.4.1 | | iPhone 11 | 15.6 | **Actions just before flickering occured:** | Event | Number of Repetitions | | ---- | ---- | | showing webView by using flutter_inappwebview | 5 | | launch app from background | 6 | | other (just using app normally) | 4 | | Total | 15 | We have not found exact way to reproduce the matter yet :cry: **Package Dependency:** almost all firebase packages, video_player, audio_service, flutter_inappwebview, and more... **Console log when flickering happened:** ``` Execution of the command buffer was aborted due to an error during execution. ``` logs looks quite same with: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1109688949 https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1134784299 https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/97910

We continue to have conversations with the Skia team on how to debug and fix this. After https://skia-review.googlesource.com/c/skia/+/566883, we should have more intelligence on what kind of command buffer errors are encountered. We are also reviewing the logic around backgrounding and interaction with command submission. In the meanwhile, I am going to review @ostk0069 s [excellent summary](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1214737544) to see if we can use use that information to add to what we know about this bug. More to come.

**Here is our additional Info about research**

We've customized our app with high load on
- CPU
- Rendering
- Memory

and monkey test the app. However, any of them did not reproduce the flickering😭 

By looking at our summary this is the high probability way to reproduce the flickering:
1. app slowdowns for some reason
2. freeze
3. make the app to background
4. make the app to foreground

> We are also reviewing the logic around backgrounding and interaction with command submission.

Just notice that our app does not have any background logic except for audio_service.

If it is possible, we would like to share the instruments log when the flickering appeared.
From a privacy point of view, could we send it by DM in twitter or something else?

This problem is the main cause of blocking our application release. 
If theres anything we could do, please let us know.
Reproduced in 3.0.5(iOS 15.6.1 iPhone13 Pro Max), hard to trigger this bug.

@chinmaygarde This issue reproduces fairly easily in our app when using an older iPhone (5s / 6), when playing back an HLS video (using the video_player package). The simplest trigger in our case is to switch between fullscreen/landscape a couple of times and back and wait for the show to start.. ;)
Happens both with a version that was built using Flutter 2.10.5 and 3.0.5.
iPhone 5s, iOS 12.5.5
[Edit] as others noted: the playback looks nice, it's everything but the player widget that flickers
Can you provide a code sample? Maybe provide private access to your repo to some key persons in the Flutter team?

> @chinmaygarde Is there any way to send event log or error when flickering occurred on customer's devices? We would like to measure flickering occurrence probability if possible.

We reviewed our Crashalytics logs for when it happened, an nothing was to be found in the logs, unfortunately.
how fix it ?
Hi, Flutter 3.3 no longer uses Skia but Impeller. Does this potentially mitigate the flickering? Has anyone reporting issues here faced the same on Flutter 3.3?

> Hi, Flutter 3.3 no longer uses Skia but Impeller. Does this potentially mitigate the flickering? Has anyone reporting issues here faced the same on Flutter 3.3?

I was telling myself this morning same thing. We will be updating to 3.3 in production soon, hopefull that solves the issue

> Hi, Flutter 3.3 no longer uses Skia but Impeller. Does this potentially mitigate the flickering? Has anyone reporting issues here faced the same on Flutter 3.3?

Flutter 3.3 still uses Skia by default, Impeller can be used only as a "feature preview", by setting some flags as mentioned [here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/wiki/Impeller). Updating to 3.3 only didn't fix the issue in my project, but there's a possibility that enabling Impeller will do the thing.

> Updating to 3.3 only didn't fix the issue in my project

How did you check the issue doesn't occur in Flutter 3.3.0?

We also need to reproduce this issue locally to be sure it hasn't been fixed in 3.3. but we don't know how to reproduce or measure the issue.

> > Updating to 3.3 only didn't fix the issue in my project
> 
> How did you check the issue doesn't occur in Flutter 3.3.0?
> 
> We also need to reproduce this issue locally to be sure it hasn't been fixed in 3.3. but we don't know how to reproduce or measure the issue.

In my case, it's a mobile application with a `GoogleMap` and it occasionally uses `InAppWebView` from [this package](https://pub.dev/packages/flutter_inappwebview). The flickering occurs on some iOS devices and in some cases if the user:
1. opens the map
2. goes to menu (that is displayed over the map) and selects an option, that opens the webview
3. closes the webview

I can try extracting a code sample from my app, but I won't be able to confirm if it allows reproducing the issue, because I can't reproduce it on my end.

> Can you provide a code sample? Maybe provide private access to your repo to some key persons in the Flutter team?

I'll see what I can do.. no promises though, the team is fully loaded at the moment.
Has anyone reporting issues here faced the same on Flutter 3.3?

A QA member of my team has reproduce the issue twice. Just to contextualize, we use a webview on part of our app, and here is what he does to get the flickering wonderful effect : 

- Launch a webview

- Grab the phone again, unlock, and go back to the app

Hi all,

There are a couple of changes to Skia that are now available on the master channel, and which we expect to be in the 3.4 beta of Flutter.

https://skia-review.googlesource.com/c/skia/+/575667 <- We suspect that this may fix a race in command buffer submission when going into the background.

https://skia-review.googlesource.com/c/skia/+/566883 <- If that change does not fix the flickering issue, then this change will give more verbose logs when command buffers fail.

@talarecc Not sure what version of Flutter you were using, but if your repro is reliable on 3.0 or 3.3 on the stable channel, it would be a big help if you could give it a shot on master channel. If you still see flickering on master channel, then if you can share the full device logs starting from slightly before the flickering starts to happen, that would be incredibly useful.

Thanks everyone for your patience on this issue. Hopefully we're getting close.

If you can make a flutter project that uses inappwebview that would still be useful, doesn't have to be just the fresh template. Doesn't have to be pretty code, either. Just whatever tickles the bug, so that we can figure out what's going on in the background.

I still have this issue in my app, i had made #97910 this year when i thought it was related to the call of several methods when the app becomes in `inactive` state. 

The pattern i had from my beta testers was : 

1. Use the app 
2. On iOS swipe up to go back to the home screen, on Android use the `backstack`
3. On iOS the app made the phone frozen few seconds (like i got with the error in the linked issue, with some GPU buffer error)
4. Then the app disappeared like during a crash but was not in terminated state
5. The user opens back the app from the phone's home screen 
6. Resuming the app after that was causing the flickering (as shown in the first comment of this issue) "forever" in this "app session"
7. Kill the app and launch it again fixes the flickering  until the next GPU error

Note : This was reproductible 1 time over a hundred and my app doesn't have any  webviews inside. 
I will attach a video next time i experience it (because i still do on `flutter 3.3`)

If you're only able to reproduce on a release version of your app, the Skia changes added os logging which we should be able to see if you reproduce, note the time it happened, then collect a sysdiagnose from the device and send it to us.  

I haven't been able to reliably collect a sysdiagnose from pressing physical buttons in a few iOS releases.  The "easiest" way I've found is to:
1. Settings > Accessibility > Touch > AssistiveTouch > Toggle on, then Single-Tap > Analytics.  When the white button appears on the screen, tap it.  You should see a notification at the top that analytics are being collected.  At that point you can turn AssistiveTouch back off.  
2. Wait ~5 minutes
3. Settings > Privacy & Security > Analytics & Improvements > Analytics Data.  Find the sysdiagnose_2022... with the matching timestamp.  Select it, choose the Share button in the top right corner.  You can Airdrop it to your Mac, or email it to yourself.  
4. Once you have the sysdiagnose on your Mac, you can peek in the logs.  Inside the sysdiagnose there is a bundle called `system_logs.logarchive`.  On the command line, run (this assumes your process is the default "Runner":
```
$ /usr/bin/log show /path/to/sysdiagnose_2022.../system_logs.logarchive --predicate "process = 'Runner'" > ~/Desktop/flutter_log.txt
```
5. Open `~/Desktop/flutter_log.txt` and confirm it contains some logging (columns with timestamps, etc), then attach it to this GitHub issue.  Make sure to also share the time you reproduced the flickering so we can narrow down where in the logs to look.

**Note sysdiagnoses contain personally identifiable information.  Be cautious sharing them.  Unless you are reproducing on a test device do not send the link to the full sysdiagnose in GitHub.**  You can reach out to us on Discord to coordinate sharing, if needed https://discord.gg/BS8KZyg.  

Thank you!

I’ve experienced this flickering several times this week on 3.3.2. I have an iOS app that stays running the background all the time because it stays connected with CoreBluetooth. It also uses the GPU/Neural Engine periodically to inference on data received over Bluetooth. CoreML will automatically use CPU while in the background and GPU/Neural Engine in the foreground. I don’t have any platform views, but since I have experienced the flickering, I am interested in the race condition fix on master.

I last experienced the flickering while confirming CPU and energy usage in Xcode while alternating between foreground and background over various periods of time while periodically receiving and inferencing. 

I have reproduced with release builds installed (and actively logging) with Xcode but didn’t see any error logs … I would like to run with the fix from last month. At this point, is the beta or master build the build to test?
